sections.title: Attempting to release the bull early

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Releasing the bull into the cow pen is a bad idea.

sections.title: Talking to Cassius again before talking to both Gillie and Seth

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you needed me to do again?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: I need you to speak to Gillie and Seth Groats. We need to learn everything we can.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: They live just across the River Lum. Head east from here and you should be able to find them.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Hello, I'm Gillie the Milkmaid. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you share what makes your cows so productive?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Absolutely! The secret is happiness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Happiness?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: The cows make the best milk when they're happy, and to be honest, they make too much!

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: I'm always glad that adventurers come by and help with milking them. I wouldn't be able to keep up otherwise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That can't be everything though, can it?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Come to think of it, I'm sure my father keeps a few tricks up his sleeve. Why don't you go and ask him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would be Seth, wouldn't it?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: It would be! I'm sure he'll he happy to share some information with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and speak to him now, thank you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've already had a chance to talk to him. He did indeed have some tricks to share!

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: That's good. You must be ready to care for your own cows at this point!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you think so, then I must be. Thank you for your help, I'll make sure that knowledge is put to good use.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Well well, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you could teach me a little about how you're so successful with your animals.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Lookin' to get into the business, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something along those lines.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Well, always happy to help another budding farmer out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! So where do I start?

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Would always recommend me Groats Principles as yer basics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Groats Principles?

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Aye. First one is that happy animals makes happy farmers.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Second of me principles is to never take more than you need. Greed'll stop you in your tracks.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: The last principle: never let the taxman know yer earnings. Money should be spent on the animals!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I've ever paid any taxes.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: You're ahead of the game then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've found a way to get around the taxes?

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: A way or two.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Anyway, feel free to grab me book on the topic from the shelf over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thank you for the help.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Well well, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you could teach me a little about how you're so successful with your animals.

lines.speaker: Seth Groats
lines.text: Mmm we already had this talk. Go read me book on the shelf over there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go grab it, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've actually got it here, I'll take it away and give it a read somewhere a little more peaceful.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: You!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Yes you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I... help you?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: That depends. Do you like making money?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not averse to making money.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: And do you enjoy... milk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Milk?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Milk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose it's okay.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Well, fair enough. If your passion for milk changes, let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't say I've thought much about it.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Would you like to think more about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I going to get from thinking more about... milk?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Profit!

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Ten to fifteen percent, performance depending.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we going to be making that profit? And who even are you anyway?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Ah, my name is Cassius. I got carried away.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: But, we're going to construct an empire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of milk?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Of milk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you intend to make an empire of milk?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: So, the first step is going to involve you doing a little... research.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: You're going to cross the River Lum, find Gillie and Seth Groats, and learn about animal husbandry.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Then... Then you're going to cross back to this side of the river, and tell me all about how they've made their animals so productive.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: It's a perfect plan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What use is it if I know everything and you don't? Couldn't I make my own... milk empire... with that information?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If anything I should be cutting you in at ten to fifteen percent.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Hey, I'm the one supplying the high-quality milk here.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Not to mention, my associate Brutus is already embedded in the Groats operation to work a different angle. That will prove most lucrative.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... I guess I'll go and see the Groatses then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not sure I would like that, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've managed to lose that sample of milk...

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: I'll give you some more once you've got some space.

sections.title: Talking to Cassius before sampling the milk

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Have you given that sample a try yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that's not good milk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's really not good milk.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: So, what do you think? The milk is fantastic, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't what I was expecting, certainly.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: We're going to change the lactic landscape! Before we do though, I have another task for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now what do you need?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: We need... a permit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shouldn't you have got a permit before making milk?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: The permit is to sell milk, not to make milk!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, where are we going to get this permit?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: From the Duke of Lumbridge! Where else? You need to bring him our milk and I'm sure he'll give us our permit without fuss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure having the Duke drink our milk is a good idea?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Of course! Soon the whole of Lumbridge will be drinking my, I mean our milk!

sections.title: Attempting to drink the second sample

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I'm ready to drink another vial of this stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should find someone else to give it to.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Has the Duke given us our permit yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, no. I could do with another sample of milk though, if you have one spare?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: I should have one somewhere here. Just a moment.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: It looks like you don't have space to carry this. You'll have to grab it from me when you have room.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: I should have one somewhere here. Just a moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, no, but I am working on it!

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: So, how did you get on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've learned a lot about how the Groats family care for their animals. They say the secret is happiness!

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Happiness?! That can't be the only trick they use! Surely you found something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that you mention it, Seth did say he had a book that I... don't have on me right now.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: The legendary Groats book on milk cultivation? I'm most interested! Go fetch it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this book from Seth. He claims it's got his principles of animal husbandry in it, if that's of any use?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Hand it here, quickly!

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Now, this is most curious.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: The idea that a happy animal will do what you want is frankly absurd. What if they choose to not produce. Are you supposed to respect that?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: However, the writings on the systems of taxation are fascinating. I couldn't imagine Seth was smart enough to put this together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, do you think this is enough to start our milk empire?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: No, no. Not quite yet. First of all, you haven't tried our milk yet, have you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I have, no. You already have some milk?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Don't look so worried. Here, have a try.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, what did you want me to do for the permit again?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Go speak to Gillie Groats. She seems to handle the milk business in Lumbridge. Maybe she'll get you set up under their permit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Hiya, did you need something else from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did. I've been trying to secure a business permit from the Duke, but he said that as it is a milk-related business venture, I should speak to you instead.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: The Duke is a good man, directing you to me! I've been running the milk side of our farm for a while now.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Were you thinking of being a competitor to us then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can prove we wouldn't be if you could just try this sample of...

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Sample of?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have misplaced my sample of milk. I'll be back when I have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think if you were to try this sample here you'd see we wouldn't be competing.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: You want me to drink a vial of mysterious liquid?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's not mysterious, it's milk!

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: No, I think you should drink it, I'll know the quality of it from your reaction.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: It is just milk after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would it convince you to help me with my permit?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: It definitely won't hurt your chances.

sections.title: Attempting to drink the sample while not next to Gillie

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shouldn't drink this unless I have Gillie nearby.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Have you tried that milk sample yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I really don't want to, but I guess I must.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that's worse milk than before.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: That looked like a horrible experience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't pleasant.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Where did you even get that milk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My business partner, Cassius. I don't know where he gets it from originally though.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Cassius... I don't recognise the name. He's not mentioned anything about its source?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, but he apparently has enough of a source to start a business. I'm mostly trying to be helpful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If the milk's bad, you're not going to recommend us for a permit, are you?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: With how bad it looks, you wouldn't be any competition to us.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: However, I wonder if I'd be doing a public service by protecting anyone else from it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything more I could do to convince you?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Now that you mention it, I've had an incredibly aggressive bull disrupting my herd lately. I've got him caught in the pen over there.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Perhaps you'd be interested in dealing with him? I'd owe you a fairly large favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A roughly permit sized favour?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: How are you getting on with the bull?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm getting there! He looks quite tough though.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Has the Duke given us our permit yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's become rather complicated, but I am working it out!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings. I come seeking a business permit.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Oh wonderful! What sort of business are you looking to start?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking to get into the milk trade!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Milk? Well, the Groats family still have their permit, and I can't start stepping on their toes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you'd just try this milk...

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I really couldn't possibly give you a permit without their approval. This is really their territory.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Why not speak to Gillie Groats? She seems to handle the milk side of the business now. Maybe she'll get you set up under their permit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're the Duke. Couldn't you just grant a permit?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: No, no. It's really quite important you speak to Gillie about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you the highest power in this land?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Don't go digging too deeply into that. Even I have some... commitments that I have to keep to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're the Duke, you shouldn't need the approval of a business entity to grant a permit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not to mention, can't they just refuse to have any competitors?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Well absolutely, but I'm sure you'll find they're more than reasonable about things.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Entirely. Completely reasonable.

sections.title: Attempting to open the gate again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brutus has already been dealt with.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: You succeeded! I'll let the Duke know that you're all good to start a milk business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think that's going to be necessary anymore.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Oh? What happened in that field?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That bull was Cassius' other business partner, Brutus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure he won't be feeling particularly friendly once I let him know.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: That bull was one of the business partners?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: I wonder if his aggression is why Cassius' herd are producing such bad milk...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm starting to think Cassius doesn't even have his own herd.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: You think he's been stealing milk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure where else he would've been getting it from.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Why don't you go and talk to him about it! He may appreciate knowing the Brutus was bad for business.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Thank you once again for handling Brutus, he was causing quite a problem for my herd.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: And, if you haven't yet spoken to Cassius, you should probably let him know what's transpired.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cassius, I don't think we'll be getting our business permit.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Why? What's happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brutus is dead.

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Dead?

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: How could this have happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the Duke asked me to... and then Gillie said that...

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: You?!

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Don't tell me you killed Brutus and ruined my- our, plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In my defence, I had no idea that was Brutus! You made it sound like he was a business partner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not to mention, apparently his aggression was ruining Seth's herd's milk. This should be a good thing!

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: Ruining the milk? The unique flavour was what I was trying to sell!

lines.speaker: Cassius
lines.text: You should leave. I can't even look at you.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Oh, just the person I wanted to see! I have something for you.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Ah, your inventory is full. Can you make some room?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: This is for helping out with that bull. Our cows are much happier these days.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: One of our local milk enjoyers gave us some enchanted cow bells.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: They seem to be quite versatile, enjoy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, thank you! I'll make sure to check it out.

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: I also have something else for you but your inventory is full. Can you make some room?

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: I also have this for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, thank you!

sections.title: Talking to Gillie again if the player doesn't have the lamp and hasn't used it yet

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: I have this for you!

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Ah, your inventory is full. Can you make some room?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, thank you!

sections.title: Talking to Gillie without a cowbell amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost that amulet you gave me...

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: Oh, no worries, here's another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Gillie Groats
lines.text: No problem.
